author: George Orwell
note: Dystopian masterpiece that explores themes of surveillance and control. Absolutely gripping.

title: Brave New World
author: Aldous Huxley
note: A fascinating exploration of a dystopian future. Different from 1984 but equally thought-provoking.

title: Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix
author: J.K. Rowling
note: The longest book in the series but worth every page.

title: Harry Potter and the Goblet of Fire
author: J.K. Rowling
note: The series gets darker and more complex. Excellent turning point.

title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
author: J.K. Rowling
note: Best book in the series. Introduces complex characters and an intricate plot.

title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
author: J.K. Rowling
note: Good continuation of the series with better world-building.

title: Harry Potter and the Sorcerer's Stone
author: J.K. Rowling
note: The book that started it all. A magical beginning.

title: Quantum Supremacy
author: John Preskill
note: Dense but interesting exploration of quantum computing's future.

title: On the Road
author: Jack Kerouac
note: Captured the spirit of the beat generation. Literary and adventurous.

title: Maus
author: Art Spiegelman
note: A powerful graphic novel about the Holocaust. Emotionally impactful.

title: Digital Minimalism
author: Cal Newport
note: Want to explore minimalist approaches to technology.
